title: Cannot store a locally build image manifest to an oci-archive
body:
Hi,
I tried to build a multiarch image using `buildah <build|bud> --manifest ...` and to store it into an oci-archive. I used `containers-storage:` as source for skopeo - somehow skopeo seems to find the manifest but then fails to read.

Confusingly if I push the manifest using `podman manifest push` to a registry I can use `skopeo copy docker:// ... oci-archive:<file>`

### How to reproduce
```bash
cat << EOF > Containerfile
FROM docker.io/library/debian:trixie-slim

RUN touch /test.txt
EOF

script=$(cat << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -x
buildah --version
skopeo --version

buildah build --manifest localhost/test:latest --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 .
skopeo copy --multi-arch all containers-storage:localhost/test:latest oci-archive:archive.tar
EOF
)

podman run --rm --interactive --tty --net=host \
    --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --privileged \
    --device /dev/fuse:rw \
    --volume ./:/workspace --workdir /workspace \
    quay.io/containers/aio:v2026.04.22 \
    bash -c "${script}"
```

### Output
```plaintext
+ buildah --version
buildah version 1.43.1 (image-spec 1.1.1, runtime-spec 1.2.1)
+ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.22.2 commit: c766fdc4c1ff9525fa6a38f860430f10646124b3
+ buildah build --manifest localhost/test:latest --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 .
[linux/arm64] STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/library/debian:trixie-slim
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian:trixie-slim...

FATA[0000] copying image 1/2 from manifest list: determining manifest MIME type for containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/run/containers/storage:overlay.imagestore=/var/lib/shared,overlay.imagestore=/usr/lib/containers/storage,overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs,overlay.mountopt=nodev,fsync=0]localhost/test:latest@002550503e5633a93a9e5b6f880d83988724eed3a6327e7d852b7ffeacd72f2c: reading manifest for image instance "sha256:752f16047579d2e88854cbe73779c54eb62b2b345e6d79db52b1b6fcd55203ca": locating item named "manifest-sha256:752f16047579d2e88854cbe73779c54eb62b2b345e6d79db52b1b6fcd55203ca" for image with ID "002550503e5633a93a9e5b6f880d83988724eed3a6327e7d852b7ffeacd72f2c" (consider removing the image to resolve the issue): file does not exist
```

# Workaround
Push the image to a registry then use skopeo copy.
```bash
cat << EOF > Containerfile
FROM docker.io/library/debian:trixie-slim

RUN touch /test.txt
EOF

script=$(cat << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -x
buildah --version
skopeo --version

buildah build --manifest localhost/test:latest --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 .
podman run --rm --name registry --detach --publish 5000:5000 docker.io/library/registry:3.1.0
podman tag localhost/test:latest localhost:5000/test:latest
podman manifest push --tls-verify=false localhost:5000/test:latest
skopeo copy --multi-arch all --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/test:latest oci-archive:archive.tar
EOF
)

podman run --rm --interactive --tty --net=host \
    --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --privileged \
    --device /dev/fuse:rw \
    --volume ./:/workspace --workdir /workspace \
    quay.io/containers/aio:v2026.04.22 \
    bash -c "${script}"
```
### Output
```plaintext
+ buildah --version
buildah version 1.43.1 (image-spec 1.1.1, runtime-spec 1.2.1)
+ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.22.2 commit: c766fdc4c1ff9525fa6a38f860430f10646124b3
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

This is ~as designed, images constructed using `{podman,buildah} manifest` can only be published using `{podman,buildah} manifest push`. They are not “ordinary images” in the sense that `{podman,buildah} manifest add` can add a _reference_ to an on-registry image, and the image is not copied into `containers-storage:`. Such an image is only understood by the `… manifest` subcommands, Skopeo does not know how to process them.

`{podman,buildah} manifest push` to `oci-archive:` might work (but I didn’t try that now).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
